title: Briwax Filler Sticks Grey – Quick Wood Repair for Scratches, Cracks & Nail Holes | Assorted Shades
features: Effortless Wood Repair: Easily conceal scratches, gouges, and imperfections with Briwax Filler Sticks, ensuring a flawless finish on your furniture
Versatile Color Matching: Twelve rich shades provide a perfect match to various wood tones, allowing you to seamlessly blend repairs for a natural look
Durable and Long-Lasting: Formulated with high-quality waxes, our filler sticks offer robust protection, enhancing the longevity of your cherished wooden pieces, ADD TO CART now
Quick and Convenient Application: The user-friendly design ensures hassle-free application, enabling both professionals and DIY enthusiasts to achieve professional-grade results
Precision Crafting: The fine-tipped design allows for precise application, making it easy to touch up small blemishes or tackle larger wood restoration projects with ease
description: From the manufacturer Wax Filler Sticks - Repair & Restore Wood Surfaces with Ease Elevate your woodworking projects with our Wax Filler Sticks, designed to effortlessly repair and restore wood surfaces. Whether you are a DIY enthusiast or a professional craftsman, these sticks offer a convenient solution for filling cracks, scratches, and imperfections in wood. Crafted from high-quality materials, our wax filler sticks provide a seamless finish that blends flawlessly with various wood tones. Easy to use and versatile, these sticks are ideal for furniture restoration, flooring repairs, and other woodworking tasks. Enhance the beauty of your wooden creations with our Wax Filler Sticks and achieve professional results every time BRIWAX Briwax makes leading luxury wood finishes in England for discerning wood lovers and furniture makers. About Us Briwax makes leading luxury wood finishes in England for discerning wood lovers and furniture makers. Henry Flack started manufacturing French polishes and blending waxes in 1860 to supply the cabinet makers and woodcarvers in East London. After severe bomb damage during the Second World War, the company relocated to Beckenham in Kent. The Bollom family acquired Briwax in 1961 and in 2013 Briwax became part of the Rustins group. The Briwax Product range has been carefully selected from the finest traditional trade finishes drawing on 150 years of experience supplying furniture manufacturers. Many of the Briwax polishes and products are based on the original formulas developed to meet the quality demands of the professional finisher. The popular original wax polish offers a simple smooth finish for either unfinished raw timber or a final touch to a polished surface and is available in 14 different colour variations
